descriptions.text: 宇宙船をパワーアップさせて、戦闘の準備を整えよう！これは、名作シューティングゲーム「R-TYPE」を完全に忠実に移植した作品です。POWアーマーを破壊すると、6種類の異なるユニットのうちの1つが現れ、それを使ってR-9をパワーアップできます。ビットを装備し、レーザーユニットを取得してフォースを出現させ、さらにパワーアップさせましょう。フォースはR-9に統合したり、分離したりすることができます。反射レーザービーム、空対地ミサイル、対空レーザーなど、武器を最大限に活用しましょう。発射ボタンを押し続けるとビームキャノンから弾を発射します。発射ボタンを押し続けてビームゲージをチャージし、放すと強力なウェーブキャノンの一撃を放つことができます！長く押し続けるほど、威力が増します！各ステージの最後には、巨大戦艦との戦いを含むN32ボスが待ち受けています。

R-Type is a 1987 horizontally scrolling shooter video game developed and published by Irem for arcades. The player controls the R-9 "Arrowhead" starship in its efforts to destroy the Bydo, a powerful alien race bent on wiping out all of mankind. The R-9 can acquire a glowing orb called a "Force", giving limited protection from enemy fire and providing additional firepower.
R-Type was the first game to run on Irem's 16-bit M72 arcade system. The development team drew inspiration from Gradius, Aliens and works by H.R. Giger. The music was composed by Masato Ishizaki, while character designs were by Akio Oyabu. The arcade version was distributed in North America by Nintendo, and was the last arcade title to be released by the company.
R-Type was commercially successful and praised by critics for its graphics and addictive gameplay, but was criticized for its difficulty. It is commonly cited as one of the best shoot 'em up games, and one of the greatest video games of all time. Its success inspired several sequels and spin-off games, as well as home ports and releases for digital distribution services. A remake of the game was released as R-Type DX for the Game Boy Color in 2000. A 3D
